display.purpose: Hero headlines on landing page and major section intros. Used once per page maximum.

headline.purpose: Page titles and primary section headers. Dashboard headings.

title.purpose: Card titles, dialog headers, subsection headings. Most common heading level.

body.purpose: All body copy, form labels, descriptions. Max 65-75ch line length in prose.

label.purpose: Badges, status indicators, metadata tags. Uppercase with wide tracking.

shadows.name: ambient-rest
shadows.purpose: Default shadow for cards at rest. Barely perceptible separation from background.

shadows.name: hover-lift
shadows.purpose: Hover state for cards and interactive surfaces. Gentle lift signaling interactivity.

shadows.name: elevated-surface
shadows.purpose: Dialogs, dropdowns, popovers. Clearly above the page plane.

shadows.name: seal-accent
shadows.purpose: Warm gold-tinted shadow for achievement badges and milestone seals only.

motion.name: transition-fast
motion.purpose: Quick feedback for small UI changes (icon color, text color).

motion.name: transition-base
motion.purpose: Standard transition for buttons, inputs, most interactions.

motion.name: transition-slow
motion.purpose: Deliberate transitions for larger movements (panel slides, shadow changes).

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Main call-to-action button for primary actions like submit, save, or confirm.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; background: #b91c1c; color: #ffffff; padding: 0.5rem 1rem; border-radius: 0.5rem; font-family: 'DM Sans', sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; border: none; cursor: pointer; transition: background 180ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1), transform 180ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #7f1d1d; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(1px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(185, 28, 28, 0.5); }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Success/approval actions, typically used by managers or for completion states.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; background: #059669; color: #ffffff; padding: 0.5rem 1rem; border-radius: 0.5rem; font-family: 'DM Sans', sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; border: none; cursor: pointer; transition: background 180ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1), transform 180ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: #047857; } .ds-btn-secondary:active { transform: translateY(1px); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(5, 150, 105, 0.5); }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: Secondary actions with less emphasis. Used for cancel, back, or alternative options.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; background: #ffffff; color: #1c1813; padding: 0.5rem 1rem; border-radius: 0.5rem; font-family: 'DM Sans', sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; border: 1px solid #e8e3d9; cursor: pointer; transition: background 180ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1), border-color 180ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-btn-outline:hover { background: #f5f3ee; border-color: #574c3d; } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(185, 28, 28, 0.5); }

components.name: Default Card
components.description: Standard container for grouped content. Used for progress steps, submissions, and information blocks.
components.html:
Pre-Schema Step 1

Complete your portfolio review

Detailed content goes here with comfortable spacing and clear hierarchy.
components.css: .ds-card { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 1rem; background: #ffffff; border-radius: 0.75rem; padding: 1.5rem; box-shadow: 0 1px 3px 0 rgba(0,0,0,0.08), 0 1px 2px -1px rgba(0,0,0,0.06); border: 1px solid rgba(28, 24, 19, 0.1); transition: box-shadow 250ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1), border-color 250ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-card:hover { box-shadow: 0 4px 6px -1px rgba(0,0,0,0.08), 0 8px 16px -4px rgba(0,0,0,0.1); } .ds-card-header { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.5rem; } .ds-card-title { font-family: 'Playfair Display', Georgia, serif; font-size: 1.953rem; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1.35; color: #1c1813; margin: 0; } .ds-card-description { font-family: 'DM Sans', sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; color: #574c3d; line-height: 1.5; margin: 0; } .ds-card-content { font-family: 'DM Sans', sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; line-height: 1.75; color: #1c1813; }

components.name: Status Badge
components.description: Status indicators for steps, submissions, and progress states.
components.html: SUBMITTEDAPPROVEDNOT STARTED
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; height: 1.25rem; padding: 0.125rem 0.5rem; border-radius: 9999px; font-family: 'DM Sans', sans-serif; font-size: 0.8rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.5; letter-spacing: 0.04em; text-transform: uppercase; white-space: nowrap; } .ds-badge-primary { background: #b91c1c; color: #ffffff; } .ds-badge-success { background: #059669; color: #ffffff; } .ds-badge-neutral { background: transparent; color: #574c3d; border: 1px solid #e8e3d9; } .ds-badge-accent { background: #ca8a04; color: #ffffff; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Standard text input for forms and data entry.
components.css: .ds-input { width: 100%; height: 2.5rem; padding: 0.5rem 0.75rem; background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #e8e3d9; border-radius: 0.5rem; font-family: 'DM Sans', sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; color: #1c1813; transition: border-color 180ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1), box-shadow 180ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-input:focus { outline: none; border-color: #b91c1c; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(185, 28, 28, 0.5); } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #a89984; } .ds-input:disabled { opacity: 0.5; background: #f5f3ee; cursor: not-allowed; }

narrative.overview:
A supportive space for professional growth where progress is celebrated with quiet dignity. This system balances academic refinement with human warmth, creating an environment that feels both prestigious and encouraging. The interface should feel like a quality educational institution that genuinely cares about each person's journey, not a bureaucratic compliance system or generic corporate LMS.

The aesthetic draws from editorial design and scholarly institutions: refined typography, warm cream backgrounds with subtle paper texture, and a restrained color palette that uses deep burgundy, forest green, and bronze gold sparingly but meaningfully. The density is comfortable, not cramped. White space and rhythm create breathing room for focused learning tasks.
keyCharacteristics: Warm cream backgrounds with subtle paper texture for editorial tactility
Serif headings (Playfair Display) paired with refined sans body text (DM Sans)
Restrained color strategy: heritage wine, growth sage, and distinction bronze used sparingly
Soft shadows that suggest elevation without heaviness
Thoughtful spacing that varies for visual rhythm, never monotonous
Subtle animations that reinforce actions without distraction

rules.name: The Restrained Accent Rule
rules.body: Heritage wine, growth sage, and distinction bronze together occupy ≤20% of any screen. Their scarcity makes each use meaningful. Default to neutrals; reach for color when it carries semantic weight (action, status, achievement).

rules.name: The Warm Neutral Rule
rules.body: Never use pure black (#000) or pure white (#fff) as defined values. Every neutral is tinted toward the brand's warm, scholarly character (chroma 0.005–0.01 in OKLCH space). This creates subliminal cohesion across the palette.

rules.name: The Hierarchy Contrast Rule
rules.body: Adjacent heading levels must differ by ≥1.25 ratio in size OR ≥200 weight units. Avoid flat scales where h2 and h3 are visually identical. Each step down should be unmistakable.

rules.name: The Line-Length Rule
rules.body: Body text in prose contexts (descriptions, instructions, feedback) is capped at 65–75 characters per line. Use max-width or container constraints. Wider measure fatigues readers; narrower measure breaks rhythm.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest unless they're interactive containers (cards, buttons) or overlay elements (modals, dropdowns). Decorative elevation is forbidden. Shadows serve functional purposes only: separating interactive surfaces or signaling hover states.

rules.name: The Ambient-Only Rule
rules.body: No directional shadows (0 10px 0 0 rgba...). All shadows are ambient, suggesting soft diffuse light from above, not harsh spotlight drama. This maintains the refined, editorial quality.

dos: Do use serif headings (Playfair Display) for all h1-h3 elements to maintain academic refinement and editorial quality.
Do vary spacing intentionally. Card padding (1.5rem) should differ from button padding (0.5rem × 1rem) should differ from section gaps (3rem+). Rhythm over uniformity.
Do cap body text line length at 65–75 characters in prose contexts (instructions, feedback, descriptions). Improves readability and creates comfortable focus.
Do use heritage wine, growth sage, and distinction bronze sparingly and semantically. Color signals meaning (action, status, achievement), not decoration.
Do tint all neutral colors toward warm (never pure black #000 or pure white #fff in definitions). This creates subliminal brand cohesion.
Do provide meaningful feedback on interactions: hover lift on cards, focus rings on inputs, subtle scale or translate on button active states.
donts: Don't use border-left or border-right greater than 1px as colored accents on cards, list items, or callouts. Side stripes are an AI/SaaS cliché explicitly rejected by this system.
Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text). All text is solid color. Emphasis via weight, size, or color, not gradients.
Don't apply glassmorphism (backdrop-blur, frosted-glass cards) decoratively. This system is flat-by-default with ambient shadows, not layered glass.
Don't use the hero-metric template (big number, small label, gradient accent, supporting stats). This is a generic SaaS pattern explicitly listed as an anti-reference.
Don't create identical card grids where every card has the same icon + heading + text layout repeated. Vary card content and structure to avoid monotony.
Don't use modals as the first solution. Exhaust inline expansion, progressive disclosure, and slide-out panels before reaching for a modal overlay.
Don't use pure sans-serif for all headings. The serif/sans pairing (Playfair Display for headings, DM Sans for body) is non-negotiable for maintaining the academic register.
Don't wrap everything in a container. Most elements can align to natural page flow. Reserve max-width containers for prose content and centered layouts.

Don't make the interface feel like corporate LMS platforms (Cornerstone, Workday) with cold bureaucratic aesthetics, generic SaaS dashboards with bland blue-and-cream schemes, or overly playful/childish designs with excessive gamification.
